LanguageData.VFEPD_AloeVera.description: A herb with succulent leaves that are arranged in a rosette. The leaves are grey to green and sometimes have white spots on their surfaces. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_AloeVera.label: aloe vera
LanguageData.VFEPD_Barley.description: A cereal grain that people can use in bread, beverages, stews, and other dishes. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Barley.label: barley
LanguageData.VFEPD_Beans.description: A herbaceous bush with a long history of cultivation. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Beans.label: beans
LanguageData.VFEPD_BellPepper.description: Also known as sweet peppers, bell peppers are technically nightshades. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BellPepper.label: bell pepper
LanguageData.VFEPD_Blueberry.description: A shrub prized for its sweet edible fruits. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Blueberry.label: blueberry
LanguageData.VFEPD_BrusselsSprout.description: A form of cabbage grown for its edible buds called "sprouts". This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BrusselsSprout.label: brussels sprout
LanguageData.VFEPD_Buckwheat.description: A pseudocereal cultivated for its grain-like seeds. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Buckwheat.label: buckwheat
LanguageData.VFEPD_ButternutSquash.description: A vine plant that produces a vegetable with a sweet, nutty taste similar to that of a pumpkin. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_ButternutSquash.label: butternut squash
LanguageData.VFEPD_Canola.description: A bright-yellow flowering variety of the rapeseed, cultivated mainly for its oil-rich seed. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Canola.label: canola
LanguageData.VFEPD_Carrot.description: A herbaceous plant of the Apiaceae family that produces an edible taproot. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Carrot.label: carrot
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cassava.description: A tuberous edible plant also called manioc, mandioca, or yuca. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cassava.label: cassava
LanguageData.VFEPD_Celery.description: A vegetable consisting of a long fibrous stalk tapering into leaves. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Celery.label: celery
LanguageData.VFEPD_Chickpea.description: One of the earliest cultivated legumes, widely grown for its nutritious seeds. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Chickpea.label: chickpea
LanguageData.VFEPD_CoconutPalm.description: A palm tree cultivated extensively in tropical areas for its edible fruit, the coconut. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_CoconutPalm.label: coconut palm
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cucumber.description: A sprawling vine with large leaves and curling tendrils, triangular prickly hairy leaves and yellow flowers. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cucumber.label: cucumber
LanguageData.VFEPD_Garlic.description: A plant with long, flat grasslike leaves forming a stalk. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Garlic.label: garlic
LanguageData.VFEPD_Healshroom.description: A slow-growing mushroom which yields herbal medicine when harvested. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Healshroom.label: healshroom
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lettuce.description: A leaf vegetable commonly eaten fresh and served as the base of green salads. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lettuce.label: lettuce
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lotus.description: Sometimes colloquially called a water lily, lotus plants are adapted to grow in the flood plains of slow-moving rivers and delta areas. This one is fake. Intended to be placed over water. Or wherever you want, I'm not your mom!
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lotus.label: lotus
LanguageData.VFEPD_Melon.description: A plant producing a sweet, edible, and fleshy fruit of considerable size. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Melon.label: melon
LanguageData.VFEPD_Moss.description: A nutrient-rich moss that can be used to feed cave animals, but not humans. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Moss.label: cave moss
LanguageData.VFEPD_Oats.description: The oat, sometimes called the common oat, is a species of cereal grain grown for its seed, which is known by the same name. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Oats.label: oats
LanguageData.VFEPD_Okra.description: A flowering plant in the mallow family valued for its edible green seed pods. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Okra.label: okra
LanguageData.VFEPD_Peanut.description: A plant that produces seeds with an oblong shape and a papery seed coat. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Peanut.label: peanut
LanguageData.VFEPD_Pineapple.description: A sweet tropical fruit with a tough leathery skin and spiky leaves on top. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Pineapple.label: pineapple
LanguageData.VFEPD_Radish.description: A tuberous edible plant also called manioc, mandioca, or yuca. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Radish.label: radish
LanguageData.VFEPD_Rye.description: A plant that grows well in much poorer soils than those necessary for most cereal grains. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Rye.label: rye
LanguageData.VFEPD_Sorghum.description: A drought resistant cereal used for food, animal fodder, the production of alcoholic beverages, and biofuels. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Sorghum.label: sorghum
LanguageData.VFEPD_Sunflower.description: A large crop used for its edible oil and edible fruits. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Sunflower.label: sunflower
LanguageData.VFEPD_SweetPotato.description: A tuberous edible plant also called manioc, mandioca, or yuca. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_SweetPotato.label: sweet potato
LanguageData.VFEPD_Taro.description: A tropical plant grown primarily for its edible corm, a root vegetable. This one is fake. Intended to be placed over water. Or wherever you want, I'm not your mom!
LanguageData.VFEPD_Taro.label: taro
LanguageData.VFEPD_Tinctshroom.description: A genetically-engineered mushroom which secretes a self-altering dye. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Tinctshroom.label: tinctshroom
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeAlmond.description: A deciduous tree cultivated for its fruit, almonds. The sweet almond can be eaten plain or be used as ingredient in desserts and confectioneries. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeAlmond.label: almond tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeAvocado.description: An evergreen tree with thick, leathery leaves that produces perfect white, ivory to yellow flowers. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeAvocado.label: avocado tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeMango.description: A deciduous tree cultivated for its fruit, almonds. The sweet almond can be eaten plain or be used as ingredient in desserts and confectioneries. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeMango.label: mango tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeOlive.description: A subtropical broad-leaved evergreen tree. Its leaves, leathery and lance-shaped, are dark green above and silvery on the underside and are paired opposite each other on the twig. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeOlive.label: olive tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_WaterChestnut.description: An aquatic vegetable that grows in marshes, under water, in the mud. This one is fake. Intended to be placed over water. Or wherever you want, I'm not your mom!
LanguageData.VFEPD_WaterChestnut.label: water chestnut
LanguageData.VFEPD_Watercress.description: Watercress is a rapidly growing, perennial plant. It is one of the oldest known leaf vegetables consumed by humans. This one is fake. Intended to be placed over water. Or wherever you want, I'm not your mom!
LanguageData.VFEPD_Watercress.label: watercress
LanguageData.VFEPD_YellowBellPepper.description: Also known as sweet peppers, bell peppers are technically nightshades. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_YellowBellPepper.label: yellow bell pepper
